mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and output expansion, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO increases, or the signal predictive power vanishes during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.5% annually forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, eroding real corporate margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold, or the signal shows no predictive alpha after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative when PAYEMS increases, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates top-down economic expansion with bottom-up consumer spending, creating a strong positive signal for S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when GDP growth decelerates while retail sales remain flat, or the interaction effect fails to outperform a simple average in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal an imminent supply-side contraction and reduced factory utilization, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5d returns in > 70% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward validation.
